title: Performance, Capacity, Efficiency, and Cost Tradeoffs · Decision Evidence Lens: Overload Policy
summary: The team optimizes synthetic speed while real users face slow devices, large payloads, queue growth, resource contention, and unpredictable bills. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 overload policy increment in capacity and cost evidence model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound queues and concurrency and define admission, degradation, prioritization, backpressure, shedding, retry, and recovery behavior.
Measure end-to-end and component behavior with controlled environments, percentiles, resource signals, variance, and profiler evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Overload Policy · Theory Overload Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Overload Policy contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Overload Policy · Theory Overload Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound queues and concurrency and define admission, degradation, prioritization, backpressure, shedding, retry, and recovery behavior. For Predict Overload Policy · Theory Overload Policy, keep Overload Policy connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Overload Policy while producing capacity and cost evidence model.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Bound queues and concurrency and define admission, degradation, prioritization, backpressure, shedding, retry, and recovery behavior. A defensible solution applies Overload Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to capacity and cost evidence model.

# Worked focus: pp2-overload-policy — Bound queues and concurrency and define admission, degradation, prioritization, backpressure, shedding, retry, and recovery behavior.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
focus: pp2-overload-policy
decision-evidence: Bound queues and concurrency and define admission, degradation, prioritization, backpressure, shedding, retry, and recovery behavior.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
